pi_items.verified_value: Olaparib (LYNPARZA)
pi_items.source: FDA, DrugBank, NCI, PubMed Central - olaparib is a PARP inhibitor approved for ovarian, breast, pancreatic, and prostate cancers

pi_items.verified_value: PARP inhibitor (Poly(ADP-ribose) polymerase inhibitor)
pi_items.source: FDA label, DrugBank, cancer.gov

pi_items.verified_value: BRCA1/2-mutated ovarian cancer (maintenance therapy post-platinum chemotherapy)
pi_items.source: SOLO1 trial (NEJM), FDA approvals, PubMed Central articles on olaparib maintenance therapy
Drug databases, PubMed - all three are real medications with documented pharmacology

pi_items.verified_value: Antiepileptic drug (AED), sodium channel blocker, first-line for partial (focal) seizures
pi_items.source: Epilepsy Foundation, StatPearls (NCBI), Medscape, WHO AED guidelines

pi_items.verified_value: Anticoagulant, vitamin K antagonist
pi_items.source: DrugBank, Merck Manual, PubMed - used for DVT/PE management

pi_items.verified_value: Anticoagulant, vitamin K antagonist
pi_items.source: DrugBank, clinical pharmacology literature - similar to warfarin, used for DVT management

pi_items.verified_value: Carbamazepine
pi_items.source: Task description explicitly identifies carbamazepine as the antiepileptic drug being evaluated; clinical justification: strong CYP3A4 inducer with contraindicated interaction

pi_items.metadata_value: CYP3A4 induction by carbamazepine substantially reduces olaparib AUC
pi_items.verified_value: Carbamazepine is a strong CYP3A4 inducer; olaparib is a CYP3A4 substrate; concomitant use reduces olaparib AUC by approximately 87% (90% CI: 84%-89%)
pi_items.source: FDA label (accessdata.fda.gov), Medscape, PubMed Central articles on olaparib-CYP3A4 interactions, clinical drug-drug interaction trials with rifampicin (similar CYP3A4 inducer)

pi_items.verified_value: 87% AUC reduction (90% CI: 84%-89%), based on data from CYP3A4 inducer trials
pi_items.source: FDA prescribing information, olaparib-rifampicin drug interaction study (PubMed), clinical pharmacology literature

pi_items.verified_value: Contraindicated/Do Not Coadminister (Red) - strong recommendation to avoid unless no suitable alternative available
pi_items.source: FDA label, Drugs.com, DrugBank interaction data, clinical guidelines for olaparib use

pi_items.verified_value: Substantial reduction in olaparib plasma exposure leads to loss of PARP inhibitor maintenance therapy efficacy in BRCA-mutated ovarian cancer
pi_items.source: Olaparib SmPC (Summary of Product Characteristics), FDA label, clinical pharmacology and oncology literature

pi_items.verified_value: BRCA1/2-mutated (both BRCA1 and BRCA2 mutations are indications for olaparib maintenance therapy)
pi_items.source: SOLO1 trial, SOLO2 trial, FDA approvals for olaparib as maintenance therapy

pi_items.verified_value: High-grade serous ovarian cancer (mentioned in README and evidence docs)
pi_items.source: Task description and evidence files

pi_items.metadata_value: BRCA1-mutated ovarian cancer patient on olaparib with epilepsy requiring antiepileptic therapy
pi_items.verified_value: Clinically plausible scenario: BRCA-mutated ovarian cancer patients on olaparib maintenance therapy can develop comorbidities (e.g., epilepsy) requiring antiepileptic therapy
pi_items.source: Medical literature on cancer patient comorbidities and PARP inhibitor use

pi_items.verified_value: Warfarin is used for DVT (deep vein thrombosis) anticoagulation therapy
pi_items.source: Merck Manual, Medscape, clinical guidelines on DVT management

pi_items.verified_value: Acenocoumarol is used for DVT anticoagulation therapy, similar to warfarin
pi_items.source: Clinical pharmacology literature, drug databases

pi_items.verified_value: Warfarin has minimal pharmacokinetic interaction with olaparib (warfarin primarily metabolized by CYP2C9 and CYP1A2, not CYP3A4)
pi_items.source: Drug interaction databases, PubMed literature on warfarin metabolism

pi_items.verified_value: Acenocoumarol has minimal pharmacokinetic interaction with olaparib (primarily metabolized by CYP2C9, weakly by CYP3A4)
pi_items.source: DrugBank, clinical pharmacology literature

privileged_info_summary: Verified: Olaparib is a PARP inhibitor used as maintenance therapy for BRCA1/2-mutated ovarian cancer following platinum-based chemotherapy. Carbamazepine is a first-line antiepileptic for partial seizures and a strong CYP3A4 inducer. Olaparib is metabolized by CYP3A4. Concomitant carbamazepine reduces olaparib AUC by approximately 87% (90% CI: 84%-89%), which is clinically contraindicated as it eliminates olaparib's therapeutic benefit. Warfarin and acenocoumarol are anticoagulants used for DVT management with minimal pharmacokinetic interaction with olaparib (not CYP3A4 substrates). Oncology nurse practitioners in gynaecological oncology routinely assess drug interactions for cancer patients requiring co-medications. The clinical scenario (BRCA-mutated ovarian cancer patient on olaparib with partial epilepsy requiring an antiepileptic) is medically plausible.
